briefing.opening: Good morning, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for July 9, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensifying Climate Crisis: Global Heatwaves and Ecosystem Impacts
sections.content: Last June was the hottest month on record in Western Europe, with a deadly heatwave featuring average temperatures 3.06°C higher, leading to over 4,700 deaths and wildfires in France and Spain [32, 35, 36, 171, 192]. This extreme climate change is also manifesting in unpredictable heavy rains and heatwaves in Korea's Incheon region [25], and is severely impacting global ecosystems, with Arctic fishing grounds shifting northward and Amazonian indigenous communities facing immense cultural and ecological losses [3, 12, 34, 172, 173]. The domestic migratory beekeeping industry is also experiencing severe dual hardships as it becomes increasingly difficult to follow the blooming of flowers due to climate change [7, 23], and experts warn that climate change and the El Niño phenomenon will further exacerbate extreme weather and economic losses [179]. European countries like France are being warned of inadequate preparedness for these climate changes [1, 29], suggesting that the world is approaching a climate crisis tipping point [183].

sections.title: 🌱 Strengthening Climate Change Adaptation and Scientific Response
sections.content: Various efforts are underway domestically to strengthen response capabilities against climate change threats. The Korea Meteorological Administration (KMA) has released 500m high-resolution climate change scenarios to support region-specific climate crisis responses [2, 9, 10]. Jeollabuk-do Province is promoting the establishment of a Disaster Safety Research Center to prepare for unpredictable climate change [6]. Haenam County is accelerating its transition to AI-based agriculture and fisheries, and is actively developing a climate-resilient agricultural cluster [30]. Uljin County is also fully committed to joint pest and disease control in response to climate change [14, 39]. Furthermore, various local governments, including the National Science Museum, Pohang City, and Chungju City, are actively operating summer vacation programs and academies to raise climate crisis awareness among future generations and explore solutions [5, 17, 18, 19, 20, 26, 28]. The government is also taking proactive measures, such as establishing an 'early warning system for jobs' to prepare for employment impacts from AI and the carbon-neutral transition, and announcing a basic plan for related industrial transformation [45, 79, 165].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Efforts Towards a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Domestic efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society are accelerating through close cooperation between national and local governments [46, 97]. Incheon City, in collaboration with the National Climate Crisis Response Committee, is leveraging marine resources such as offshore wind power and blue carbon to declare its goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2045 and becoming a global climate-leading city, establishing a concrete roadmap [48, 52, 53, 57, 59, 60, 63, 64, 68, 71, 81, 84, 85, 87, 88, 93, 95, 96, 98, 102]. Various local governments, including Gwangmyeong City and Gongju City, are promoting the establishment of region-specific green infrastructure, such as green remodeling of public health centers, operating carbon-neutral experience programs in nature learning and healing gardens [49, 67, 74, 78, 80, 82, 103], and introducing the 'Tanso Moa Tantan-e' platform for practicing carbon neutrality in daily life [89] [58, 77]. In the corporate sector, private sector participation remains active, with Korea Southern Power accelerating greenhouse gas reduction and Sanggye Paik Hospital being selected as an excellent hospital for carbon-neutral activities [31, 43, 56, 101]. However, as revealed by the 'bare face' of Cheongju, a city advocating for carbon neutrality, through its garbage bags, encouraging citizen participation and lifestyle changes for actual carbon reduction remains a crucial challenge [91].

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy Transition and Technological Innovation
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is accelerating globally, and its economic viability is also being secured, with 90% of new renewable energy sources becoming cheaper than fossil fuels [115]. Korea is strengthening energy transition cooperation with Mongolia, promoting renewable energy expansion and technology policy exchanges [106, 114, 117, 128, 154, 157]. KOTRA and the Korea Energy Agency are actively pursuing overseas market leadership, including dispatching trade delegations to target Australia's 2GW offshore wind power bidding market [108, 111, 113, 116, 120, 127, 131, 132, 133, 135, 145, 147, 153, 161, 164]. Domestically, Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power (KHNP) has developed water electrolysis operation technology that can accommodate the variability of renewable energy even without ESS (Energy Storage Systems), thereby enhancing the hydrogen era and renewable energy utilization [124, 137, 138, 150, 152, 156, 163]. Corporate efforts to implement RE100 are also continuing, with SK Chemicals setting a goal to use 100% renewable energy by 2032, and Kumho Tire achieving a renewable energy ratio of 13% [112, 126, 143, 151, 155]. However, as seen in Europe, as the proportion of renewable energy increases, power grid and storage infrastructure can experience bottlenecks [144], and social consensus is needed on conflicting issues with renewable energy expansion policies, such as the controversy over the construction of a new gas power plant in the Jeju region [121, 134, 140].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing demonstrates that amidst the globally intensifying climate crisis, scientific responses both domestically and internationally, accelerated transitions to carbon neutrality, and efforts in renewable energy innovation are all progressing simultaneously. We hope that these multifaceted efforts will lead to practical solutions for overcoming the climate crisis. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: Prime Minister Han Sook-jung presided over the National Policy Coordination Meeting held at the Seoul Government Complex on the 9th. Reporter Kim Jeong-geun. On the 9th, the government unveiled its first master plan to address job crises resulting from industrial transitions, including AI transformation (AX) and carbon neutrality transformation (GX). The government defined industrial transition as 'a process of fostering new jobs, not eliminating existing ones,' and emphasized the state's role in ensuring workers do not solely bear the burden of employment insecurity.
